items.title: Mbappé (knee) out of Madrid's game with Benfica
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé has not been named in Real Madrid's squad for their Champions League playoff second leg with Benfica

items.title: FIFA: 'Confidence' Mexico can host despite unrest
items.summary: FIFA are monitoring the situation in Mexico but have "full confidence" the country can host World Cup games amid the recent unrest and violence.

items.title: Sources: McKennie close to new Juventus contract
items.summary: United States men's midfielder Weston McKennie is on the verge of signing a new contract with Juventus, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Celta-legende Aspas (38) overtreft Villa in La Liga en nadert Messi
items.summary: Iago Aspas bevindt zich in de late herfst van zijn carrière, maar is het scoren nog lang niet verleerd. De clubicoon van Celta de Vigo was zondag goud waard voor de Galiciërs tegen Real Mallorca. Dankzij twee goals overtrof Aspas David Villa en op een ander recordlijstje is hij op weg naar Lionel Messi.

items.title: F1 contracts: How long is each driver signed up for?
items.summary: A look at how long each Formula 1 driver's contract is ahead of the 2026 F1 season where several changes could take place for 2027.

items.title: Man Utd post profit but debt rises to £1.3bn
items.summary: The amount Manchester United owe increases to almost £1.3bn in outstanding transfer fees, rolling credit facility and legacy debt.

items.title: Vormdip brengt Groningen niet uit balans: 'Vorig jaar had PSV er even last van'
items.summary: Het zit FC Groningen de laatste weken niet mee. Door een serie van vijf nederlagen op rij is De Trots van het Noorden naar de negende plaats gezakt. Dick Lukkien vertelt wat hem houvast geeft en wijst op een vergelijkbare vormdip bij andere Eredivisie-clubs.

items.title: Champions League's biggest upsets: Bodo/Glimt join list of giant-killers
items.summary: Norwegian minnows Bodo/Glimt pulled off one of the biggest-ever UCL shocks. Here are 10 more.

items.title: Don't talk about winning: The secret behind Bodo/Glimt's European success
items.summary: Bodo/Glimt's Champions League win vs. Inter Milan is a major upset. The signs were there in last year's Europa League run.

items.title: 'Juventus moet het opnieuw van Koopmeiners hebben' in jacht op Galatasaray
items.summary: Teun Koopmeiners staat met Juventus voor een loodzware opdracht in de return tegen Galatasaray, het beslissende tweede Champions League duel in het tweeluik. Na de 5-2 nederlaag in de heenwedstrijd in Istanbul moeten de Italianen in eigen huis alles rechtzetten. Volgens de Italiaanse sportkrant La Gazzetta dello Sport wacht Juventus dan ook een bijna onmogelijke klus. In buitenlandse media gaat de aandacht vooral uit naar enkele bepalende spelers uit die eerste ontmoeting, onder wie Koopmeiners zelf, Noa Lang en Victor Osimhen.

items.title: Sheffield Wednesday takeover collapses as would-be buyers end their interest
items.summary: Consortium included Dunfermline co-owner James Bord They say due diligence showed their offer was far too high A consortium seeking to buy Sheffield Wednesday says it has withdrawn from the process. The consortium, which features James Bord, the co-owner of Dunfermline, was granted exclusivity by club administrators in December. However, it has released a statement confirming that interest is at an end. A spokesperson for the consortium said: “Throughout the process to purchase Sheffield Wednesday Football Club we have at all times respected the integrity of the process, working professionally, constructively and transparently with the administrator, the EFL and the IFR to provide them with the details they need to satisfy their tests. Continue reading...

items.title: Consortium seeking to buy Sheff Wed withdraws interest
items.summary: A consortium seeking to buy Sheffield Wednesday says it has withdrawn from the process.

items.title: AZ hielp Meerdink met creatief Dubai-tripje in revalidatie
items.summary: Mexx Meerdink heeft een deel van zijn revalidatie in Dubai afgewerkt. De spits was daar aanvankelijk alleen op vakantie, maar AZ dacht mee en stuurde een fysiotherapeut die kant op. Zo kon Meerdink in een gunstig klimaat werken aan zijn comeback.

items.title: Chelsea and West Ham fined over 'mass confrontation'
items.summary: Chelsea and West Ham are fined £325,000 and £300,000 respectively for a "mass confrontation" between players in their match in January.

items.title: Messi betreurt 'verspilde' tijd op school: 'Ik dacht: wat een idioot ben ik'
items.summary: Lionel Messi baalt ervan dat hij in zijn jeugdjaren niet genoeg aandacht heeft besteed aan school, met name aan het leren van Engels. Dat laat de achtvoudig Ballon d'Or-winnaar weten in de Mexicaanse podcast Miro de Atras.

items.title: Constitution Hill ruled out of Champion Hurdle as hurdling career ends
items.summary: Constitution Hill has been ruled out of running in the Champion Hurdle at the Cheltenham Festival by trainer Nicky Henderson.

items.title: Bodø/Glimt bask in ‘crazy’ Champions League victory over shellshocked Inter
items.summary: Italian giants were well beaten by side who dazzled despite Norway’s domestic season not starting until next month There was a moment after the final whistle at San Siro on Tuesday night when the head coaches, Bodø/Glimt’s Kjetil Knutsen and Inter’s Cristian Chivu, stood chatting, seemingly discussing some tactical element of the game that had just finished. Chivu appeared genuinely interested in what Knutsen had to say, smiling politely, but above all he looked utterly bemused. What the hell had just happened? His Inter team, top of Serie A by 10 points and undefeated in the league since 23 November, had not only lost the home leg of their Champions League playoff against the Norwegian side but been well beaten: 2-1 on the night and 5-2 on aggregate. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘I hurt so much for years but now feel proud’: John Quansah on the pain of a football career ruined by injury
items.summary: Quansah left Ghana for Ajax as a boy but injury ended his career before it started. He now earns £5 a day as a builder and strives to find a new purpose in life By The Blizzard John Quansah looks at a glass display case hanging on the wall of his living room in Obuasi, Ghana. Inside are three trophies from his days as a youth player at Ajax. For years, they lay tucked away in the back of a cupboard, but two years ago, that changed. “I’m an adult now,” John says. “It’s time to look at the past differently. When I look at the trophies now, I don’t just feel pain. I am grateful too – for those beautiful years.” Of course, he didn’t fulfil his big dream. But not everyone can say they have played for Ajax. He has every reason to be proud, to look back at that time with satisfaction. During a move, he finds the trophies again and decides to mount a display case on the wall of his new living room. Inside, he places three trophies. One for the best player at a youth tournament in Belgium. Next to that, one from another competition, and one he received for sportsmanship, also awarded in Belgium. Continue reading...

items.title: 'No stomach for a relegation fight' - fiery O'Hara analyses Spurs' plight
items.summary: Ex-Tottenham midfielder Jamie O’Hara has slammed the attitude and mentality of the Spurs squad and believes they “haven’t got the stomach for a relegation fight”.

items.title: England to host Ukraine at Everton's Hill Dickinson
items.summary: The Lionesses will host Ukraine in their Women's World Cup qualifier at the Hill Dickinson Stadium in June.
